name: Czochralski Growth
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: A single silicon crystal is grown by slowly pulling a seed from molten silicon, producing the large-diameter, highly pure monocrystalline ingots that power most commercial solar cells.

name: Czochralski Growth
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: A single silicon crystal is grown by slowly pulling a seed from molten silicon, producing the large-diameter, highly pure monocrystalline ingots that power most commercial solar cells.

name: Float Zone Process
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: A radio-frequency coil melts a narrow zone of a silicon rod and sweeps it end-to-end, yielding ultra-pure, defect-free monocrystalline silicon with the lowest impurity levels achievable.

name: Float Zone Process
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: A radio-frequency coil melts a narrow zone of a silicon rod and sweeps it end-to-end, yielding ultra-pure, defect-free monocrystalline silicon with the lowest impurity levels achievable.

name: Monocrystalline Pull
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: Controlling the pull rate and melt temperature during Czochralski growth aligns silicon atoms into a single continuous crystal lattice, minimising grain-boundary recombination losses.

name: Monocrystalline Pull
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: Controlling the pull rate and melt temperature during Czochralski growth aligns silicon atoms into a single continuous crystal lattice, minimising grain-boundary recombination losses.

name: Ingot Sawing
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: Diamond-wire sawing slices monocrystalline ingots into thin wafers with minimal kerf loss, directly determining cell thickness and the amount of silicon consumed per watt produced.

name: Ingot Sawing
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: Diamond-wire sawing slices monocrystalline ingots into thin wafers with minimal kerf loss, directly determining cell thickness and the amount of silicon consumed per watt produced.

name: CIGS Co-Evaporation
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Copper, indium, gallium and selenium are simultaneously evaporated onto a substrate in high vacuum, forming a polycrystalline absorber whose bandgap can be tuned by adjusting the Ga:In ratio.

name: CIGS Co-Evaporation
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Copper, indium, gallium and selenium are simultaneously evaporated onto a substrate in high vacuum, forming a polycrystalline absorber whose bandgap can be tuned by adjusting the Ga:In ratio.

name: Chalcopyrite Growth
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: CIGS belongs to the chalcopyrite crystal family; its I-III-VI₂ structure gives it a direct bandgap of ~1.1–1.2 eV and a very high absorption coefficient, allowing efficient absorption in just 1–2 µm of material.

name: Chalcopyrite Growth
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: CIGS belongs to the chalcopyrite crystal family; its I-III-VI₂ structure gives it a direct bandgap of ~1.1–1.2 eV and a very high absorption coefficient, allowing efficient absorption in just 1–2 µm of material.

name: CdTe Sputtering
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Cadmium telluride thin films are deposited by sputtering or close-spaced sublimation; with a near-ideal 1.45 eV bandgap, CdTe cells rival CIGS in thin-film efficiency at lower manufacturing cost.

name: Thin Film CIGS
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Thin-film CIGS technology uses 100× less semiconductor material than silicon wafers, enabling lightweight, flexible modules that can conform to curved surfaces and building-integrated applications.

name: Thin Film CIGS
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Thin-film CIGS technology uses 100× less semiconductor material than silicon wafers, enabling lightweight, flexible modules that can conform to curved surfaces and building-integrated applications.

name: Perovskite Ink
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: Metal-halide perovskite dissolved in solvent can be inkjet-printed or blade-coated at room temperature, offering a radical reduction in fabrication energy compared with high-temperature silicon processing.

name: Perovskite Ink
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: Metal-halide perovskite dissolved in solvent can be inkjet-printed or blade-coated at room temperature, offering a radical reduction in fabrication energy compared with high-temperature silicon processing.

name: ABX₃ Crystal
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: The ABX₃ perovskite crystal structure (A = organic cation, B = Pb/Sn, X = halide) gives exceptional defect tolerance and a tunable bandgap from ~1.2 to 2.3 eV by halide substitution.

name: Spin-Coat Perovskite
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: Spin-coating perovskite precursor solutions produces highly uniform thin films in seconds; anti-solvent drip during spinning promotes rapid crystallisation and large grain sizes that reduce recombination.

name: Lead-Free Perovskite
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: Replacing lead with tin, bismuth or antimony eliminates toxicity concerns; though efficiency is currently lower, lead-free perovskites are essential for regulatory acceptance in consumer products.

name: Tandem Perovskite
description: Slot Perovskite junction (×2.0) into your prototype.
long_description: Stacking a wide-bandgap perovskite top cell (~1.7 eV) on a silicon bottom cell allows each sub-cell to absorb a complementary slice of the solar spectrum, breaking the single-junction Shockley–Queisser limit.

name: III-V Epitaxy
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: III-V compound semiconductors (GaAs, InGaP, Ge) grown by molecular beam epitaxy or MOCVD can be stacked with angstrom-precision lattice matching, enabling record efficiencies above 45% under concentrated sunlight.

name: GaAs MOCVD
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: Metal-organic chemical vapour deposition grows atomically thin III-V layers by thermally decomposing precursor gases; the precise thickness control is critical for tuning sub-cell bandgaps in multi-junction stacks.

name: Multi-Junction Stack
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: A two- or three-junction III-V cell splits the solar spectrum so each junction absorbs near its bandgap energy, dramatically reducing thermalisation losses that limit single-junction cells to ~33% efficiency.

name: Space-Grade Cell
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: GaAs multi-junction cells dominate spacecraft power systems because of their superior radiation hardness, high specific power (W/kg), and stable performance across the extreme temperature swings of orbit.

name: MgF₂ Coating
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: A quarter-wavelength magnesium fluoride layer deposited by vacuum evaporation reduces front-surface reflection from ~30% to below 5%, one of the earliest and still-used anti-reflection strategies.

name: MgF₂ Coating
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: A quarter-wavelength magnesium fluoride layer deposited by vacuum evaporation reduces front-surface reflection from ~30% to below 5%, one of the earliest and still-used anti-reflection strategies.

name: ZnS ARC Layer
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Zinc sulphide's refractive index (~2.3) provides good impedance matching between silicon and air; ZnS ARC layers can be deposited at low temperature, making them compatible with thin-film substrates.

name: ZnS ARC Layer
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Zinc sulphide's refractive index (~2.3) provides good impedance matching between silicon and air; ZnS ARC layers can be deposited at low temperature, making them compatible with thin-film substrates.

name: Anti-Reflect Film
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Single-layer anti-reflection coatings exploit thin-film interference: the film thickness is chosen so reflected waves from top and bottom interfaces cancel destructively, boosting transmitted photon flux.

name: Anti-Reflect Film
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Single-layer anti-reflection coatings exploit thin-film interference: the film thickness is chosen so reflected waves from top and bottom interfaces cancel destructively, boosting transmitted photon flux.

name: Sol-Gel ARC
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Porous silica coatings grown from sol-gel precursors achieve near-zero reflectance across a broad wavelength range through a graded refractive index profile that smoothly bridges silicon and air.

name: Sol-Gel ARC
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Porous silica coatings grown from sol-gel precursors achieve near-zero reflectance across a broad wavelength range through a graded refractive index profile that smoothly bridges silicon and air.

name: PECVD SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Plasma-enhanced chemical vapour deposition of silicon nitride is the industry standard for front-surface passivation; the hydrogenated film passivates dangling bonds and provides excellent anti-reflection at ~80 nm thickness.

name: PECVD SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Plasma-enhanced chemical vapour deposition of silicon nitride is the industry standard for front-surface passivation; the hydrogenated film passivates dangling bonds and provides excellent anti-reflection at ~80 nm thickness.

name: Hydrogenated SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Hydrogen atoms incorporated during PECVD diffuse into the silicon bulk on firing, passivating iron and other metal impurities that act as recombination centres in multicrystalline wafers.

name: Hydrogenated SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Hydrogen atoms incorporated during PECVD diffuse into the silicon bulk on firing, passivating iron and other metal impurities that act as recombination centres in multicrystalline wafers.

name: Plasma Nitride
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Radio-frequency or microwave plasma dissociates SiH₄ and NH₃ at low temperatures (~300–400 °C), depositing a stoichiometry-tunable SiNₓ:H film that simultaneously provides optical and chemical passivation.

name: Silicon Nitride
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: SiNx has a refractive index between 1.9 and 2.3 (tunable by Si:N ratio), an optical bandgap of ~5 eV, and a fixed positive charge that field-effect passivates n-type emitter surfaces.

name: Silicon Nitride
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: SiNx has a refractive index between 1.9 and 2.3 (tunable by Si:N ratio), an optical bandgap of ~5 eV, and a fixed positive charge that field-effect passivates n-type emitter surfaces.

name: ALD Alumina
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: Atomic layer deposition of Al₂O₃ grows a perfectly conformal, pinhole-free film one monolayer at a time by alternating trimethylaluminium and water pulses, achieving sub-nm thickness control at the wafer scale.

name: ALD Alumina
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: Atomic layer deposition of Al₂O₃ grows a perfectly conformal, pinhole-free film one monolayer at a time by alternating trimethylaluminium and water pulses, achieving sub-nm thickness control at the wafer scale.

name: Al₂O₃ Passivation
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: Aluminium oxide carries a high density of fixed negative charge (~10¹³ cm⁻²) that repels minority-carrier holes from p-type surfaces, providing outstanding field-effect passivation for PERC rear contacts.

name: Thermal ALD
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: Thermal (non-plasma) ALD of Al₂O₃ at 150–250 °C produces a stoichiometric, dense film compatible with temperature-sensitive substrates while delivering the same excellent interface passivation as plasma ALD.

name: Rear Surface Al₂O₃
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: Depositing Al₂O₃ on the cell rear reduces recombination at the silicon–contact interface, replacing the conventional full Al-BSF and enabling rear local contacts that reflect more light back into the absorber.

name: Field-Effect Passiv.
description: Slot Al₂O₃ optical layer (×1.45) into your prototype.
long_description: The fixed negative charge of Al₂O₃ creates an inversion layer at the silicon surface, repelling minority carriers from the interface without needing to chemically saturate every dangling bond—field-effect passivation in action.

name: SiO₂/TiO₂ Stack
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Alternating layers of low-index SiO₂ and high-index TiO₂ form a multilayer interference stack that suppresses reflection below 1% across the entire visible spectrum, outperforming any single-layer coating.

name: Advanced Passivation
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Combined chemical (H-termination) and field-effect (charge layer) passivation strategies reduce surface recombination velocity below 1 cm/s, approaching the intrinsic limit set by Auger recombination.

name: MoO₃ Interlayer
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Molybdenum trioxide acts as a hole-selective contact due to its deep work function (~6.9 eV), enabling dopant-free carrier-selective junctions that simplify processing and avoid diffusion-related defects.

name: Optical Stack
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: A multi-layer optical stack engineered by thin-film transfer-matrix modelling simultaneously minimises reflection, maximises absorption in the active layer, and provides rear internal reflection across a broad wavelength range.

name: Rear Contact Laser
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Laser ablation opens local openings in the rear passivation layer with micron precision, allowing aluminium paste to contact the silicon only where needed and leaving the rest of the surface passivated.

name: Rear Contact Laser
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Laser ablation opens local openings in the rear passivation layer with micron precision, allowing aluminium paste to contact the silicon only where needed and leaving the rest of the surface passivated.

name: PERC Process
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Passivated Emitter and Rear Cell architecture adds a dielectric rear passivation layer with local contact openings, reducing rear recombination and boosting Voc by ~10 mV over conventional Al-BSF cells.

name: PERC Process
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Passivated Emitter and Rear Cell architecture adds a dielectric rear passivation layer with local contact openings, reducing rear recombination and boosting Voc by ~10 mV over conventional Al-BSF cells.

name: Passivated Emitter
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Passivating the heavily doped n⁺ emitter surface with a thin dielectric reduces Auger and surface recombination in the region of highest carrier concentration, improving both Voc and blue response.

name: Passivated Emitter
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Passivating the heavily doped n⁺ emitter surface with a thin dielectric reduces Auger and surface recombination in the region of highest carrier concentration, improving both Voc and blue response.

name: Local BSF Contact
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Small aluminium-alloyed local back-surface field regions provide ohmic contact while the surrounding dielectric passivates the rear, capturing most of the BSF benefit with far lower recombination area.

name: Local BSF Contact
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Small aluminium-alloyed local back-surface field regions provide ohmic contact while the surrounding dielectric passivates the rear, capturing most of the BSF benefit with far lower recombination area.

name: Bifacial Screen Print
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Transparent or grid rear contacts allow bifacial cells to absorb both direct sunlight from the front and reflected albedo light from the ground, increasing energy yield by 5–30% depending on site conditions.

name: Bifacial Screen Print
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Transparent or grid rear contacts allow bifacial cells to absorb both direct sunlight from the front and reflected albedo light from the ground, increasing energy yield by 5–30% depending on site conditions.

name: Transparent Rear
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: A transparent conductive oxide rear electrode replaces opaque aluminium, allowing rear illumination; combined with a reflective backsheet, it creates an optical cavity that boosts total light absorption.

name: Transparent Rear
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: A transparent conductive oxide rear electrode replaces opaque aluminium, allowing rear illumination; combined with a reflective backsheet, it creates an optical cavity that boosts total light absorption.

name: Double Glass Module
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Encapsulating the cell between two glass sheets protects the transparent rear contact and allows true bifacial operation, while increasing module lifetime by reducing moisture ingress.

name: Albedo Harvest
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Rear-side energy capture depends on ground reflectance (albedo): white gravel or snow-covered ground (~0.8) can add 25%+ to annual yield compared with dark soil (~0.1).

name: Albedo Harvest
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Rear-side energy capture depends on ground reflectance (albedo): white gravel or snow-covered ground (~0.8) can add 25%+ to annual yield compared with dark soil (~0.1).

name: a-Si:H Interlayer
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: An intrinsic amorphous silicon buffer layer grown by PECVD at < 200 °C passivates the crystalline silicon surface with near-zero interface defect density, enabling the record open-circuit voltages of HJT cells.

name: a-Si:H Interlayer
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: An intrinsic amorphous silicon buffer layer grown by PECVD at < 200 °C passivates the crystalline silicon surface with near-zero interface defect density, enabling the record open-circuit voltages of HJT cells.

name: Heterojunction
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: The HJT cell sandwiches crystalline silicon between thin amorphous silicon layers; the abrupt bandgap discontinuity creates built-in fields that separate carriers without doping the absorber bulk.

name: Intrinsic Layer
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: The undoped (i) a-Si:H layer between the c-Si absorber and doped contact layers provides chemical passivation without the parasitic absorption of thicker doped films, maximising infrared current.

name: TCO Front Contact
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Transparent conductive oxides (ITO, AZO) replace silver-grid fingers across part of the front surface, reducing shading; their work function is tuned to form a low-resistance contact to the a-Si:H hole layer.

name: Silver Nanowires
description: Slot HJT contact grid (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: A percolating network of silver nanowires embedded in a polymer matrix provides a flexible, highly transparent front electrode with sheet resistance < 10 Ω/□, enabling HJT cells on bendable substrates.

name: IBC Architecture
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Interdigitated Back Contact cells move all metal contacts to the rear surface, eliminating front shading entirely and allowing a full-area textured front optimised purely for light capture.

name: Interdigitated Back
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Alternating p⁺ and n⁺ diffused regions on the rear collect holes and electrons respectively; their interdigitated pattern minimises the lateral carrier travel distance to the nearest contact.

name: Zero Front Shadow
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: With no front metallisation, the IBC cell achieves the highest possible short-circuit current density for a given absorber thickness, making it the architecture of choice for high-concentration and space applications.

name: Back-Contact Cell
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: All carrier collection at the rear demands extremely long carrier diffusion lengths (> wafer thickness), requiring high-purity float-zone silicon and excellent bulk lifetime—prerequisites that IBC cells uniquely exploit.

name: Solar Tracker
description: +20% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Single- or dual-axis trackers rotate panels to follow the sun, increasing energy yield by 20–40% over fixed-tilt systems by maintaining near-normal incidence throughout the day.

name: Solar Tracker
description: +20% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Single- or dual-axis trackers rotate panels to follow the sun, increasing energy yield by 20–40% over fixed-tilt systems by maintaining near-normal incidence throughout the day.

name: Solar Tracker
description: +20% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Single- or dual-axis trackers rotate panels to follow the sun, increasing energy yield by 20–40% over fixed-tilt systems by maintaining near-normal incidence throughout the day.

name: Solar Tracker
description: +20% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Single- or dual-axis trackers rotate panels to follow the sun, increasing energy yield by 20–40% over fixed-tilt systems by maintaining near-normal incidence throughout the day.

name: Smart Inverter
description: +15% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Modern grid-tied inverters implement maximum power point tracking at the module level, provide reactive power support, and communicate real-time performance data, improving both yield and grid stability.

name: Smart Inverter
description: +15% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Modern grid-tied inverters implement maximum power point tracking at the module level, provide reactive power support, and communicate real-time performance data, improving both yield and grid stability.

name: Smart Inverter
description: +15% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Modern grid-tied inverters implement maximum power point tracking at the module level, provide reactive power support, and communicate real-time performance data, improving both yield and grid stability.

name: Smart Inverter
description: +15% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Modern grid-tied inverters implement maximum power point tracking at the module level, provide reactive power support, and communicate real-time performance data, improving both yield and grid stability.

name: MPPT Controller
description: +12% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Maximum Power Point Tracking algorithms (Perturb-and-Observe, Incremental Conductance) continuously adjust the electrical operating point to extract the maximum available power under varying irradiance and temperature.

name: MPPT Controller
description: +12% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Maximum Power Point Tracking algorithms (Perturb-and-Observe, Incremental Conductance) continuously adjust the electrical operating point to extract the maximum available power under varying irradiance and temperature.

name: MPPT Controller
description: +12% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Maximum Power Point Tracking algorithms (Perturb-and-Observe, Incremental Conductance) continuously adjust the electrical operating point to extract the maximum available power under varying irradiance and temperature.

name: MPPT Controller
description: +12% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Maximum Power Point Tracking algorithms (Perturb-and-Observe, Incremental Conductance) continuously adjust the electrical operating point to extract the maximum available power under varying irradiance and temperature.

name: Bifacial Mount
description: +10% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Elevated racking systems with optimised row spacing and ground albedo maximise rear-side irradiance for bifacial modules, with energy gains of 5–30% depending on site reflectance.

name: Bifacial Mount
description: +10% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Elevated racking systems with optimised row spacing and ground albedo maximise rear-side irradiance for bifacial modules, with energy gains of 5–30% depending on site reflectance.

name: Bifacial Mount
description: +10% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Elevated racking systems with optimised row spacing and ground albedo maximise rear-side irradiance for bifacial modules, with energy gains of 5–30% depending on site reflectance.

name: Dust Storm
description: Target player's farm output is halved next turn.
long_description: Soiling by airborne dust can cut module output by 1–40% depending on climate; arid regions with high sandstorm frequency require aggressive cleaning schedules or anti-soiling coatings to maintain yield.

name: Dust Storm
description: Target player's farm output is halved next turn.
long_description: Soiling by airborne dust can cut module output by 1–40% depending on climate; arid regions with high sandstorm frequency require aggressive cleaning schedules or anti-soiling coatings to maintain yield.

name: Dust Storm
description: Target player's farm output is halved next turn.
long_description: Soiling by airborne dust can cut module output by 1–40% depending on climate; arid regions with high sandstorm frequency require aggressive cleaning schedules or anti-soiling coatings to maintain yield.

name: Grid Failure
description: Target player skips their score phase next turn.
long_description: Transmission outages, grid instability or inverter trips take entire solar farms offline; resilience requires islanding capability, fast reconnection protocols and robust communication with grid operators.

name: Grid Failure
description: Target player skips their score phase next turn.
long_description: Transmission outages, grid instability or inverter trips take entire solar farms offline; resilience requires islanding capability, fast reconnection protocols and robust communication with grid operators.

name: Patent Block
description: Block a random research area for target player.
long_description: Intellectual property disputes over key cell architectures or manufacturing processes have repeatedly delayed technology commercialisation, forcing rivals to license or invent around dominant patents.

name: Patent Block
description: Block a random research area for target player.
long_description: Intellectual property disputes over key cell architectures or manufacturing processes have repeatedly delayed technology commercialisation, forcing rivals to license or invent around dominant patents.

name: Hail Damage
description: Destroy target player's most recently built unit.
long_description: Large hailstones can crack glass covers and shatter cell fronts; IEC 61215 hail testing (25 mm ice balls at 23 m/s) sets the minimum standard, but extreme events exceed this threshold.

name: Policy Subsidy
description: Draw 2 extra cards immediately.
long_description: Feed-in tariffs, investment tax credits and renewable portfolio standards have historically driven rapid solar deployment by improving the economics of projects that would otherwise be marginal.

name: Regulatory Change
description: Target player discards their top hand card.
long_description: Shifts in import tariffs, building codes or grid connection rules can abruptly alter project economics, creating uncertainty that affects financing costs and investment decisions across the industry.

name: Quantum Dot Array
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: Semiconductor nanocrystals confine electrons in all three dimensions, allowing precise bandgap tuning through size alone and enabling intermediate-band absorption of photons too low-energy for conventional cells.

name: Nanowire Forest
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: Arrays of vertically aligned semiconductor nanowires decouple light absorption from carrier collection, permitting efficient use of low-quality material and enabling III-V cells on cheap silicon substrates.

name: Perovskite/Si Tandem
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: A solution-processed perovskite top cell (~1.7 eV) bonded to a commercial silicon bottom cell has demonstrated efficiencies exceeding 33%, pointing toward a manufacturable path beyond the silicon efficiency ceiling.

name: Upconversion Layer
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: Rare-earth upconverter materials absorb two sub-bandgap infrared photons and emit one higher-energy photon that the solar cell can use, potentially harvesting energy that would otherwise pass straight through.

name: Hot Carrier Cell
description: Slot GaAs Multi-Jct junction (×2.8) into your prototype.
long_description: Conventional cells lose carrier kinetic energy as heat within picoseconds; hot-carrier designs extract carriers before thermalisation, theoretically pushing single-junction efficiency above 65%.

name: Micro-Textured Surface
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Sub-micron surface texture created by laser or chemical etching traps light through multiple total-internal-reflection events, increasing effective optical path length by 4n² (the Lambertian limit) within the absorber.

name: Light Trapping Grid
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Periodic photonic structures or diffraction gratings couple oblique diffraction orders into guided modes within the absorber, enabling near-Lambertian light trapping in films thinner than the carrier diffusion length.

name: Broadband ARC
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: A chirped multilayer ARC or moth-eye nanostructure graded refractive index profile suppresses reflection from UV to near-IR, capturing photons across the full terrestrial solar spectrum.

name: Self-Cleaning Coating
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: A superhydrophobic or photocatalytic TiO₂ outer layer causes water droplets to bead and roll off, carrying dust with them, maintaining optical transmission without manual cleaning in field installations.

name: plasmonic Enhancer
description: Slot Advanced optical stack (×1.65) into your prototype.
long_description: Gold or silver nanoparticles scatter incident light into high-angle modes that increase the effective absorption path in the semiconductor, particularly boosting near-infrared response near the bandgap.

name: TOPCon Contact
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Tunnel Oxide Passivated Contact uses a ~1.5 nm SiO₂ tunnel layer capped by doped polysilicon; majority carriers tunnel through while minority carriers are blocked, achieving near-perfect contact passivation.

name: Tunnel Oxide Passiv
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: The ultra-thin SiO₂ tunnel layer in TOPCon is grown by wet chemical oxidation or UV ozone, providing a reproducible, sub-2 nm oxide that balances tunnelling transparency with minority-carrier blocking.

name: Back Junction Front
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Relocating the p-n junction to the rear of the wafer means photogenerated minority carriers travel toward the high-field junction rather than away from it, reducing bulk recombination requirements.

name: Poly-Si Passivated
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Heavily doped polycrystalline silicon acts simultaneously as a contact and a surface passivant in TOPCon; its high conductivity carries current laterally to the metal fingers without compromising the tunnel-oxide barrier.

name: SHJ Contact Scheme
description: Slot IBC contact grid (×1.9) into your prototype.
long_description: Silicon Heterojunction contacts combine the passivation excellence of a-Si:H with the lateral conductivity of TCO, achieving simultaneously low contact resistance and near-zero surface recombination velocity.

name: Battery Storage
description: +18% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Lithium-ion or flow batteries store daytime surplus for evening dispatch, shifting solar energy to peak demand periods and allowing farms to participate in ancillary service markets.

name: Optimized Wiring
description: +8% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Careful string configuration, conductor sizing and minimised series resistance in DC cabling reduce resistive losses, which can otherwise consume 2–5% of generated energy in large utility-scale arrays.

name: Supply Chain Issue
description: Target player skips their score phase next turn.
long_description: Shortages of polysilicon, silver paste, or specialised glass—as seen in 2021–22—delay module delivery by months, raise spot prices and force developers to redesign projects around available materials.

name: Flooding
description: Target player's farm output is halved next turn.
long_description: Extreme rainfall and flooding can inundate tracker drives, corrode ground-mount foundations and short-circuit underground wiring, causing extended outages that eliminate months of projected revenue.

name: Equipment Failure
description: Destroy target player's most recently built unit.
long_description: Inverter faults, transformer failures or connector arcing are the most common causes of unplanned downtime in utility-scale plants; robust O&M programmes and spare-parts inventories limit revenue loss.

name: Seed Crystal Pull
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: A precisely oriented seed crystal initiates Czochralski growth, defining the crystal axis of the entire ingot and ensuring uniform electrical properties across every wafer sliced from it.

name: Wafer Etch Back
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: Alkaline or acidic etching removes saw damage from wafer surfaces, reducing surface recombination velocity and revealing a smooth, low-defect silicon face ready for further processing.

name: Necking Process
description: Slot Mono-Si junction (×1.3) into your prototype.
long_description: The Dash necking technique shrinks the crystal diameter immediately after seeding to eliminate threading dislocations, ensuring the grown ingot is truly dislocation-free throughout.

name: Selenization
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Metal precursor layers (Cu, In, Ga) are annealed in a selenium-rich atmosphere to form the CIGS absorber, offering a scalable alternative to co-evaporation for large-area deposition.

name: Co-Sputtered CIGS
description: Slot CIGS junction (×1.6) into your prototype.
long_description: Simultaneous sputtering of Cu, In and Ga targets followed by selenization precisely controls composition across large substrates, enabling roll-to-roll manufacturing of flexible CIGS panels.

name: SiO₂ Single Layer
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: A thin thermally grown silicon dioxide layer serves as both an anti-reflection coating and a surface passivant, reducing interface states that would otherwise trap and recombine photogenerated carriers.

name: Thermal Oxide ARC
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: High-temperature oxidation of silicon grows a precisely controlled SiO₂ layer whose thickness determines the destructive-interference wavelength, offering excellent uniformity at the cost of thermal budget.

name: Spin-On Glass ARC
description: Slot Basic ARC optical layer (×1.15) into your prototype.
long_description: Liquid silica precursors are spin-coated and cured to form a glass-like ARC; the approach is low-cost and compatible with large-area substrates including flexible polymer films.

name: Dual-Layer SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: A two-layer SiNx stack—one optimised for passivation chemistry, the other for optical thickness—decouples the competing requirements and achieves both low surface recombination and minimal reflection.

name: Remote Plasma SiNx
description: Slot SiNx Passivation optical layer (×1.30) into prototype.
long_description: Generating the plasma remotely from the substrate prevents ion bombardment damage, enabling higher-quality passivation on delicate surfaces such as ultrathin wafers or heterojunction structures.

name: Selective Emitter
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: A lightly doped emitter under the passivated area minimises Auger recombination while a heavily doped region beneath the metal contact reduces contact resistance—separating the competing doping requirements.

name: Fine Line Print
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: Narrower screen-printed silver fingers (< 30 µm) reduce shading losses on the front surface; advances in paste rheology and mesh technology enable finer lines without sacrificing conductivity.

name: Al Paste Rear
description: Slot PERC contact grid (×1.2) into your prototype.
long_description: A full-area aluminium rear contact forms a p⁺ back-surface field during firing that repels minority electrons from the rear and provides a low-resistance ohmic contact to the p-type base.

name: Clear Rear Glass
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Low-iron tempered glass on the module rear is highly transparent to the near-IR wavelengths reflected from typical ground surfaces, maximising photon capture from albedo illumination.

name: High Albedo Mount
description: Slot Bifacial contact grid (×1.4) into your prototype.
long_description: Tilt angle, row spacing and mounting height are co-optimised with ground albedo to maximise rear irradiance; raised tracker mounts over white gravel can exceed 30% bifacial gain.

name: String Wiring
description: +6% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Connecting cells in series strings balances voltage and current for inverter compatibility; optimal string length minimises mismatch losses while keeping cable runs within acceptable resistance limits.

name: Bypass Diode
description: +8% total farm output (permanent).
long_description: Diodes wired in parallel with sub-strings prevent hot-spot heating when cells are shaded; without bypass diodes, a single shaded cell can dissipate the power of the entire string as heat.

name: Corporate Espionage
description: Block a random research area for target player.
long_description: Theft of proprietary process recipes, characterisation data or customer contracts has been documented in the solar industry, particularly during rapid capacity ramp-ups where first-mover advantages are enormous.
